Step 1: Belief Item Distributions

Nicole Cooper

2026-07-06

Full library of response scales used across the Step 1 instrument (copied from 02_descriptives_step1_pilot.Rmd — keep in sync if that file’s scale levels ever change).

Function

Before plotting a new item, check Output/03_correlations_intent.csv (r_composite, p_composite) and Output/04_promising_beliefs.csv (pct_at_strongest, small_n_flag, ceiling_flag) for that variable — the small_n_flag in particular means fewer than 30 people sit at the “strongest” position, so any PTG number for that item is unreliable even though the raw distribution below is still accurate.

Examples

Approval of federal funding for university research (govtfund_1)

r_composite = .358, p < .001. 55.9% already at “Strongly approve” — a strong correlate that’s ceiling-limited.

Research advancing knowledge is necessary even without immediate benefits (research_4_8)

r_composite = .137, p = .018. 58.3% at “Strongly agree” — correlated but weaker, also ceiling-limited.

University researchers are motivated primarily by profit (gotvfund_6_5)

r_composite = -.027, p = .64 (n.s.). 20.3% at “Strongly disagree” (the outcome-aligned pole). Not correlated, moderate prevalence.

Researchers make mistakes and results are incorrect — likelihood (research_6.2_1)

r_composite = -.108, p = .061 (borderline n.s.). Only 5.3% at “Very unlikely” (n=16) — small_n_flag = TRUE, so treat any PTG figure for this item as unreliable.

I think about the impact of science on my daily life (research_4_4)

r_composite = .46, p < .001 — the strongest correlate in the survey. Only 19.3% at “Strongly agree” (n=58). Correlated + headroom: a genuine high-value target.

Priority: Making a profit (govtfund_7_1)

r_composite = -.026, p = .656 (n.s.). Nearly uniform spread across all 5 priority levels (12.7% / 24.0% / 23.3% / 15.0% / 25.0%) — genuine disagreement in the sample, but it doesn’t track intent to talk/share/contact reps.

Adding a new item

  1. Find the variable name and exact question wording in UnivNews_Step1_Survey_Reference.md (or the labels vectors in 02_descriptives_step1_pilot.Rmd).
  2. Confirm which lvl_* scale it uses (check the matching bar_matrix/bar_single call in 02_descriptives_step1_pilot.Rmd).
  3. Optionally pull r_composite/pct_at_strongest/flags from Output/03_correlations_intent.csv and Output/04_promising_beliefs.csv.
  4. Copy one of the chunks above and swap in the new col, question, levels, and out_file.
